user_query:
I’m organizing a community-run evening digital skills workshop in our neighborhood because the official plan feels out of touch with our reality. Please adapt the provided municipal outline into something we can actually deliver after work hours, and coordinate the essentials so we can move without waiting on bureaucratic delays.

Inputs (read all files under input/):
- input/template_curriculum.yaml — base curriculum and placeholder schedule from the municipality.
- input/proposed_outline.md — the official write-up we’re deviating from.
- input/community_feedback.md — notes from residents on what they want and what doesn’t work.
- input/volunteer_availability.csv — volunteers, skills, and when they can help.
- input/constraints.yaml — must-follow constraints for days, times, session length, and language.

Deliverables (write all outputs under output/):
1) output/meeting_notes.md — meeting notes and action items we can share with neighbors and volunteers. Include:
   - A Decisions section that clearly states: allowed days/time windows used; which modules are included (and which are optional); final delivery mode; and session language.
   - A Differences vs Proposed Outline section listing at least three concrete differences compared to input/proposed_outline.md (e.g., timing changes, module adjustments, offline-first delivery), so it’s obvious how we diverged from the official approach.
   - A Risks and Mitigations section with at least two risks taken verbatim or paraphrased from input/community_feedback.md and a brief mitigation for each.
   - An Action Items section with at least six actionable bullets. Each bullet must end with an owner tag in the form [owner: Full Name] where the name appears in input/volunteer_availability.csv. Use at least three different owners across the list. Make items concrete (e.g., “Print offline handouts for Module X”, “Check projector and power strips”, “Prepare sample spreadsheets for practice”).

2) output/curriculum.yaml — a modified curriculum configuration derived from input/template_curriculum.yaml that satisfies input/constraints.yaml and community needs. Requirements:
   - Keep the original file structure style but output a complete, standalone YAML file.
   - Set delivery_mode to "offline-first" and session_language to the value from input/constraints.yaml.
   - Ensure the module list includes exactly one module named "CV Writing" (90 minutes) and one named "Basic Excel" (120 minutes). If these are not present in the template, add them; if overlapping content exists (e.g., spreadsheets), keep both as separate modules.
   - Keep "Government E-Services Portal" in the module list but mark it optional: true and place it last in module order.
   - Create a sessions list of exactly the number specified by sessions_required in input/constraints.yaml. Each session must include: session_id, day (day of week only), start_time, end_time, room (use the room_preference from constraints), module (one module per session), assigned_volunteer (full name).
   - Every session must be scheduled on a day listed in allowed_days and wholly within one allowed_time_ranges interval, and must not exceed max_session_length_minutes.
   - Assign an available volunteer for each session whose available_days and available_time_ranges in input/volunteer_availability.csv match the session day and time window, and whose skills are relevant to the module where possible (e.g., Excel → volunteer with “Excel” or “Spreadsheets”; CV Writing → “CV Writing” or “Job Search”; Internet safety → “Internet Safety”).
   - Include a top-level field location_capacity copied from input/constraints.yaml.

3) output/email_to_center_manager.txt — a plain-text email draft to the community center manager requesting room allocation and basic support. Requirements:
   - Include a Subject line starting with: "Request: Evening Digital Skills Workshop Room Booking and Support".
   - To: manager@localcenter.example
   - CC: list the volunteer emails for the assigned volunteers (pull email addresses from input/volunteer_availability.csv for those you assigned in output/curriculum.yaml).
   - In the body, summarize the final schedule as bullet points (day, start–end time, module, assigned volunteer) consistent with output/curriculum.yaml; state that delivery is offline-first due to unreliable internet; mention that "Government E-Services Portal" is optional and scheduled last; and request access to the specified room, projector, and power strips on those days/times.
   - End with a line noting that output/curriculum.yaml and output/meeting_notes.md are attached for reference.

General constraints:
- Do not modify the files in input/; produce all edits as new files under output/.
- Ensure all volunteer names and emails you reference are present in input/volunteer_availability.csv.
- Ensure all session days/times conform to input/constraints.yaml.
- Keep the tone respectful but pragmatic; we want to move fast without waiting on slow approvals.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
